User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Risabu Connect ERP system, linked to an external authentication system and assigned a specific role and department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firebaseUid.description: Unique ID from the external Firebase Authentication system, linking this user profile to an authenticated account.

email.description: The primary email address of the user.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the ERP system (e.g., 'Admin', 'Staff').

department.description: The department the employee belongs to (e.g., 'ICT', 'Finance', 'Registrar').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

Program.title: Program
Program.description: Represents an academic program or course offered by Risabu Technical Training College.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Program entity.

name.description: The official name of the academic program or course.

description.description: A brief description of the program, including its objectives and content.

durationMonths.description: The typical duration of the program in months.

tuitionFee.description: The base tuition fee for the program.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the program record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the program record was last updated.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled or applying to Risabu Technical Training College.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the student.

lastName.description: The last name of the student.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email address for the student.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the student.

admissionDate.description: The date the student was admitted to the college.

admissionStatus.description: The current status of the student's admission (e.g., 'Applied', 'Accepted', 'Enrolled', 'Rejected', 'Deferred').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the student record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the student record was last updated.

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Represents a student's enrollment in a specific academic program for a particular period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student. (Relationship: Student 1:N Enrollment)

programId.description: Reference to the Program. (Relationship: Program 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: The date the student officially enrolled in the program.

academicYear.description: The academic year for which the student is enrolled (e.g., '2023-2024').

status.description: The current status of the enrollment (e.g., 'Active', 'Completed', 'Withdrawn', 'Suspended').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents a financial invoice issued to a student for tuition, fees, or other charges.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student to whom the invoice is issued. (Relationship: Student 1:N Invoice)

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment related to this invoice, if applicable. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N Invoice)

issueDate.description: The date the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

totalAmount.description: The total amount due on the invoice.

outstandingAmount.description: The remaining amount outstanding on the invoice.

status.description: The current payment status of the invoice (e.g., 'Issued', 'Partially Paid', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Cancelled').

description.description: A brief description of what the invoice is for (e.g., 'Tuition Fee - Fall 2024').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records a payment made by a student towards an invoice.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice this payment is applied to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N Payment)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who made the payment. (Relationship: Student 1:N Payment)

paymentDate.description: The date the payment was received.

amount.description: The amount of the payment made.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Cash', 'Bank Transfer', 'Mobile Money', 'Card').

transactionReference.description: A unique reference number for the payment transaction, if available.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was last updated.

ExpenseCategory.title: ExpenseCategory
ExpenseCategory.description: Defines categories for college expenses to facilitate organization and reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExpenseCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the expense category (e.g., 'Salaries', 'Utilities', 'Office Supplies', 'Maintenance').

description.description: A detailed description of the expense category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense category record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense category record was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Records an individual institutional expense incurred by Risabu Technical Training College.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

categoryId.description: Reference to the ExpenseCategory this expense belongs to. (Relationship: ExpenseCategory 1:N Expense)

description.description: A detailed description of the expense.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the expense.

expenseDate.description: The date when the expense was incurred or paid.

receiptUrl.description: An optional URL or path to a scanned copy of the expense receipt.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this expense. (Relationship: User 1:N Expense)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The 'userId' is the Firebase Authentication UID. This path ensures direct, secure access to a user's own profile data based on their authenticated UID.

Dedicated collection for administrative roles. The existence of a document here (where 'userId' is a Firebase UID) grants 'Admin' permissions. This provides authorization independence for global role checks.
Dedicated collection for staff roles. The existence of a document here (where 'userId' is a Firebase UID) grants 'Staff' permissions. This provides authorization independence for global role checks.
Stores academic programs offered by the college. Managed by Staff/Admins.
Stores student profiles. Includes denormalized 'firebaseUid' for direct student self-access authorization if the student is a system user.
Stores student enrollments in programs. Includes denormalized 'studentFirebaseUid' for authorization independence, allowing students to access their own enrollment records.

Stores financial invoices issued to students. Includes denormalized 'studentFirebaseUid' for student self-access and 'recordedByUserFirebaseUid' for staff ownership/auditing, ensuring authorization independence.

Stores payment records made by students. Includes denormalized 'studentFirebaseUid' for student self-access and 'recordedByUserFirebaseUid' for staff ownership/auditing, ensuring authorization independence.
Stores categories for institutional expenses. Managed by Staff/Admins.
Stores individual institutional expenses. Includes denormalized 'recordedByUserFirebaseUid' for staff ownership/auditing, ensuring authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Risabu Connect ERP prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability through strict adherence to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Dedicated Role Collections:** Global roles ('Admin', 'Staff') are implemented using dedicated collections: `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_staff/{userId}`. The mere existence of a document in these collections, where `userId` is the authenticated user's Firebase UID (`request.auth.uid`), grants the corresponding role. This completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the `/users` collection to determine a user's role, making role-based authorization atomic, highly performant, and immune to issues in batched writes or transactions. This directly supports QAPs by allowing rules to grant read/write access based on an explicit, easily verifiable role, rather than attempting to filter results based on complex logic.
2.  **Denormalized `firebaseUid` for Ownership/Responsibility:** To prevent `get()` calls when checking ownership or responsibility for specific documents, critical `firebaseUid` references are denormalized into the relevant documents:
    *   `Student` documents (`/students/{studentId}`) will include a `firebaseUid` field if the student is also a Firebase user, allowing a student to directly access their own profile.
    *   `Enrollment` documents (`/enrollments/{enrollmentId}`) will include `studentFirebaseUid` to enable students to view their own enrollments without accessing the `Student` document first.
    *   `Invoice` documents (`/invoices/{invoiceId}`) will include `studentFirebaseUid` (for student self-service) and `recordedByUserFirebaseUid` (for staff ownership/auditing, ensuring authorization independence).
    *   `Payment` documents (`/payments/{paymentId}`) will include `studentFirebaseUid` and `recordedByUserFirebaseUid` for similar reasons as invoices.
    *   `Expense` documents (`/expenses/{expenseId}`) will include `recordedByUserFirebaseUid` to allow the recording staff member to manage their own expenses, and for admins to oversee all.
    This denormalization ensures that security rules can perform direct attribute checks (`resource.data.studentFirebaseUid == request.auth.uid` or `resource.data.recordedByUserFirebaseUid == request.auth.uid`) without cross-document reads. For `list` operations, this allows secure, query-based filtering where the client provides the `firebaseUid` in the query, and the rule verifies that the queried `firebaseUid` matches `request.auth.uid`.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
Each primary collection (e.g., `/programs`, `/students`, `/expenseCategories`) is designed to contain documents that share a similar security posture. This means that rules applied at the collection level are simpler and more consistent. For instance, all documents in `/programs` can be managed by 'Staff' and 'Admin' roles, while individual `invoices` can be read by the associated student AND managed by 'Staff'/'Admin'. This approach moves complexity from granular rule logic to structural organization, improving clarity and debuggability.

**Access Modeling & Data Clarity:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** `/users/{userId}` uses the Firebase UID directly as the document ID, making `request.auth.uid` checks trivial for user-specific data.
*   **Clear References:** Relationships between entities (e.g., `Enrollment` to `Student` and `Program`) are maintained through `id` fields (e.g., `studentId`, `programId`), while authorization context is provided by the denormalized `firebaseUid` fields.
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Entities like `Student` (`admissionStatus`), `Enrollment` (`status`), and `Invoice` (`status`) explicitly use a single `status` field, promoting predictable data states.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{programId}`), and standard fields like `createdAt`, `updatedAt` are used consistently.

This robust design ensures that security rules will be concise, understandable, and highly performant, enabling scalable and debuggable backend operations for Risabu Connect ERP.
